word_list.word: (till|until) all hours (of the day and night|)
word_list.pronunciation: tˈɪl ʌntˈɪl ˈɔːl ˈaɪʊɹz ʌvðə dˈeɪ ænd nˈaɪt

senses.definition: used to refer to a really late hour at night or early morning

examples.x: We stayed up till all hours of the night, discussing our dreams and aspirations.
She worked on her research project till all hours of the day and night, determined to meet the deadline.
The party at their house went on till all hours, with music and laughter filling the night.
The café was open until all hours, catering to night owls and insomniacs with a craving for coffee.
He studied till all hours to prepare for the important exam, leaving no stone unturned in his preparation.

senses.definition: too late or close to the end of a particular period or situation to be useful or effective

examples.x: It's late in the day to start studying for the exam; you should have begun earlier.
The team is discussing potential changes, but it might be late in the day to implement them before the project deadline.
She realizes it's late in the day to pursue a new career path, given her current commitments.
They discovered the issue late in the day, after most of the work had already been completed.
He made the decision late in the day, and it had significant consequences for the outcome of the project.
We realized late in the day that the market had shifted, and our product was no longer in demand.
It will be late in the day to negotiate the terms of the contract once the deadline approaches.

senses.definition: a time that is too late for something

examples.x: I finally decided to invest in cryptocurrencies, but it seems like I'm a day after the fair as prices have already skyrocketed.
They realized the potential of the new technology, but their competitors had already implemented it, leaving them a day after the fair.
She found out about the job opening after the application deadline had passed, feeling like she's a day after the fair.
He discovered the stock market opportunity too late and ended up feeling like he was a day after the fair.
We learned about the discounted sale only after it had ended, leaving us a day after the fair for the great deals.
They had a brilliant business idea, but by the time they started working on it, the market was saturated, making them a day after the fair.
If we don't act quickly, our chance to secure funding for the project may slip away, leaving us a day after the fair.

senses.definition: a distant moment or period in the future or past that is often marked by significant technological, cultural, or historical changes

examples.x: The next total solar eclipse visible from this location is light years away, not occurring for another century.
Reflecting on the first moon landing, it seems like it happened light years away in time, though it was just over 50 years ago.
Predicting what life will be like for future generations, say, a thousand years from now, can feel like speculating about a time light years away from our own.

senses.definition: at a very early time in the morning when the Sun is just rising

examples.x: I wake up at the crack of dawn every day to go for a refreshing run.
The fishermen set out on their boats at the crack of dawn to catch the best fish.
She starts her workday at the crack of dawn to maximize productivity.
The farmers harvested the crops at the crack of dawn before the heat of the day.
He finished his painting just as the sun rose at the crack of dawn.

senses.definition: at an hour of the morning that is really early

examples.x: I always wake up bright and early to have a productive start to my day.
The team members arrive bright and early for their morning meetings.
She typically begins her workout bright and early to energize herself for the day ahead.
We woke up bright and early yesterday to catch the sunrise at the beach.
The construction crew started their work bright and early yesterday morning.
He arrived at the conference bright and early and secured a front-row seat.
Tomorrow, we will rise bright and early to catch the early morning flight.
They plan to set up the picnic area bright and early for the family gathering next week.
By next month, I aim to establish a habit of starting my workday bright and early every morning.

senses.definition: the time of the morning when the Sun has just started to shine..
at the earliest time that it is possible or suitable for someone to do something

examples.x: Please let me know if you can attend the meeting at your earliest convenience.
We kindly request that you submit the report at your earliest convenience.
Could you please review and provide feedback on the proposal at your earliest convenience?
I will send you the revised contract to sign at your earliest convenience.
The project deliverables should be submitted at your earliest convenience.
If you could kindly provide the requested information at your earliest convenience, we can proceed with the application process.

senses.definition: used to indicate that something should have happened or been done earlier

examples.x: It's about time you start taking your health seriously and prioritize exercise.
She tells her friend, "It's about time we visit that new restaurant everyone's been talking about."
It was about time he realized the importance of saving money and started budgeting.
We finally went on that long-awaited vacation—it was about time we took a break.
Tomorrow, it will be about time to submit the project that we've been working on for months.
By next month, it will be about time for us to move into our new house.

senses.definition: at the last moment that it is still possible to do something

examples.x: We are in the eleventh hour of negotiations, and a resolution must be reached soon.
The team is working frantically in the eleventh hour to meet the project deadline.
He completed the assignment at the eleventh hour, just before the submission deadline.
He would have missed the train if he hadn't arrived at the station in the eleventh hour.

senses.definition: used to refer to early morning hours or the hours after the midnight

examples.x: I often find myself writing poetry in the small hours of the morning.
She usually stays up studying until the small hours.
They frequently have deep conversations during the small hours of the night.
Last night, I stayed awake reading a captivating novel into the small hours.
He worked diligently on the project until the small hours of the morning.
We reminisced about our childhood memories during the small hours of the night.
Tomorrow, I will be finishing up my assignment in the small hours before the deadline.

word_list.word: a day late and a dollar short
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ dˈeɪ lˈeɪt ænd ɐ dˈɑːlɚ ʃˈɔːɹt

senses.definition: something that appears or arrives too late to be of any use or benefit

examples.x: They frequently find themselves a day late and a dollar short in submitting their assignments.
Yesterday, I realized I was a day late and a dollar short in applying for the scholarship.
We realized we were a day late and a dollar short in joining the exclusive club.
Tomorrow, I will likely be a day late and a dollar short in booking a hotel for the vacation.
They will probably find themselves a day late and a dollar short in responding to the job offer.

senses.definition: right after the finalization of a deal, agreement, etc.
the final moment or deadline to do something

examples.x: I always tend to finish my assignments at the last minute.
He often waits until the last minute to make important decisions.
They frequently experience a rush of adrenaline when completing tasks at the last minute.
She waited until the last minute to buy concert tickets, but luckily there were still some available.
We realized we had forgotten an essential item for the trip at the last minute.
They will likely be scrambling to finish their project at the last minute.
